title: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)

overview.description: Tender marinated chicken cooked in a rich tomato, butter and lightly spiced sauce. Based on the classic Punjabi dish from Delhi.

ingredients: 800g boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into large pieces
1 tbsp crushed kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves)
method: Add the chicken and coat thoroughly.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Grill, barbecue or cook the chicken in a very hot frying pan until lightly charred but not fully cooked through.
Heat the ghee over a medium heat.
Cook the onion for 10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the Kashmiri chilli, cumin and coriander.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the butter, cream and kasuri methi.
Add the chicken and any resting juices.
Simmer gently for 10–15 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked.

learning.did-you-know: Butter Chicken was created in Delhi in the 1950s as a way to use leftover tandoori chicken by simmering it in a rich tomato and butter sauce.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to marinate chicken with yoghurt, build a classic Punjabi curry base and balance tomatoes, butter and cream without overpowering the spices.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: One of the family's favourite curries and an excellent introduction to authentic North Indian cooking.
chapman-tips: Chicken thighs remain much juicier than breast after marinating.
Kasuri methi is one of the defining flavours of authentic Butter Chicken and is worth keeping in the pantry.
Don't skip charring the chicken—it adds the characteristic tandoori flavour.
Blend the sauce for the smooth texture found in traditional Murgh Makhani.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze the finished curry in family-sized portions.
notes.leftovers: Often tastes even better the following day as the spices continue to develop.
